record.text:
This is accurate & not hyperbole from @jfallows.bsky.social 

With support from USAID, the DRC had built an outbreak preparedness & response system “ready to go,” e.g., surveillance, labs, health workers, & stockpiles of personal protective equipment

When Musk killed USAID, that system collapsed 😢

value.text:
Future historian department:

-Front page of today's NYT reports creation of world's first trillionaire.

-Lead op-ed of same NYT is about worst-ever Ebola outbreak, which *this VERY SAME trillionaire* enabled, with his "chainsaw" destruction of US public health in the world.

The book writes itself

record.text: To my friends in tech: if you're not happy with how your bosses are behaving, this is an effective thing you can do about it.

external.description: Help power the grassroots organizing that will defeat the MAGA agenda!
external.title: Donate to Indivisible Action

value.text: For the first time in two years, we have missed a monthly fundraising goal. We can absolutely weather a slow month — we’re not about to start posting like the sky is falling — but over time (and especially heading into the midterms), we need to make up some lost ground. Please pitch in if you can!

external.title: Donate to Indivisible Action

record.text:
It's all kayfabe, and you can tell because none of these firms are putting forward, or supporting, model legislation that would outlaw data brokers:

infrequently.org/2025/08/apple-vs…

external.description: An industry of hundreds of registered data brokers maintains files on every American adult; identity graphs bind the files to people with near-total coverage; a marketplace layer makes the result quer...
external.title: Data Brokers: Unregulated Forensic Analysis — No One's Happy

record.text:
When I bang on (and on) about the unbridled cynicism of Big Tech regarding privacy -- *particularly* Apple -- this is my comparison point. You can't win this game by buying a new phone, only through demanding giants lobby for change, or at least stop opposing it:

nooneshappy.com/article/data-bro…

external.title: Data Brokers: Unregulated Forensic Analysis — No One's Happy

record.text:
📚 A book, a bench and a beverage.
I've been listening to A LOT of @lennysan.bsky.social Podcast and the latest one w/ Tony Fadell made me do this. Can't wait to go through BUILD, as his work was responsible for some of my purchases: owned both the Gen 1 iPod and iSight... Fun in the Toronto sun ☀️.

images.alt: A black woman smiling holding a glass of champagne.

record.text:
Ten years; lots of no’s; endless networking; huge doubts; countless red eyes in middle seats; and 100k words later: the smile of a woman who just sent the final pages of the completed manuscript of her first traditionally published book to her editor!🥹

“Leading with Empathy For Dummies” drops 9/29!

external.description: NSF dismantling Ocean Observatories Initiative as very strong El Niño forms. Edson, Robbins, McLean: an irreparable blind spot in U.S. climate observation.
external.title: The US Is Pulling Up a $386 Million Climate Ocean Sensor Network

record.text:
Insane self-blinding of the US. They are throwing away their investment in the ocean observation system and the benefits it provides. 
The only explanation I can think of: they don’t want the people to know what their fossil emissions are doing to our oceans.
easternherald.com/2026/06/13/u...

external.title: The US Is Pulling Up a $386 Million Climate Ocean Sensor Network

Everything JS knows, now CSS knows.
external.title: Prop For That · June 13, 2026

record.text:
Prop For That 📣

An a-la-carte #JS library for #CSS developers that writes meaningful realtime CSS variables to an element:

- value of an input
- pointer position
- size of scrollbar(s)
- colors in an image/video
- element's visibility
- tons more…

nerdy.dev/prop-for-that

external.title: Prop For That
external.description: Everything JS knows, now CSS knows.

source.title: nerdy.dev — Adam Argyle
source.description: Web design & development tips & tricks: CSS, JS, HTML, Design, & UX.

Progressively enhanced data-dense layout with grid-lanes www.projectwallace.com/blog/using-g...
